Preprocessing: high pass filter (12th order Butterworth at 1 kHz); low pass filter (12th order Butterworth at 16kHz)
DetectorDetails: trigger frequency filter for click detector module: 2 kHz threshold (high pass 6th order Butterworth); Basic Click Detector parameters were kept at default values
ClassifierDetails: PAMGuard classified details in Appendix Table B4 of paper; R script filtering based on click rate in 10 or 30s period
ManualReview: all recordings labeled with “presence” visually and aurally assessed using Adobe Audition

Comments: PAMguard click detector also used, but as described in Miller and Miller 2018

ManualReview: examine 10 s spectrograms (FFT 1024, 0.5 overlap), 0 to 30 kHz frequency range and listening to sections of interest
examine 10 s spectrogram (FFT 2000, 75% overlap, bandwidth 40 kHz)

ClassifierDetails:
Macaulay, 2020: cross correlation of  clicks' normalised average spectrum to a species-specific spectrum template
CNN: uses 5s spectrograms (viewing 1.2-19.1 kHz), implemented in Python, using PyTorch
PerformanceDetails:
PAMGuard click detector alone: 0.452 false positive rate
MHT: accuracy >= 82.6%; FDR <= 0.11 for sites used for training
DL: accuracy >= 79.4%; FDR <= 0.12 for sites used for training
true positive rate: 96%; false positive rate: 1% (hourly)

ManualReview: examine 10 s spectrogram (FFT 2000, 75% overlap, bandwidth 40 kHz)

DetectorDetails: 1 h window; resolution: 5 s (500 spectra, 10 ms Hann-windowed frames); 100 Hz
ManualReview: When echolocation signals were notable in the LTSA the sequence was inspected more closely
